revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '==沙本之大闇見戸売=== 記紀に登場する。[[開化天皇]]の皇子・[[日子坐王]]の妻、[[狭穂彦王]]、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃「狭穂...'
revision.text: ==沙本之大闇見戸売=== 記紀に登場する。[[開化天皇]]の皇子・[[日子坐王]]の妻、[[狭穂彦王]]、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃「[[狭穂姫]]」の母 彼女の母、[[建国勝戸女]]は、春日の建国勝戸女と呼ばれており、この大闇見戸女が率いる狭穂氏は当時、佐保から春日地方一帯の機内のかなり広い範囲を支配する相当な力をもった豪族だったと考えられている

revision.comment: /* 沙本之大闇見戸売= */
revision.text: ==沙本之大闇見戸売== 記紀に登場する。[[開化天皇]]の皇子・[[日子坐王]]の妻、[[狭穂彦王]]、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃「[[狭穂姫]]」の母 彼女の母、[[建国勝戸女]]は、春日の建国勝戸女と呼ばれており、この大闇見戸女が率いる狭穂氏は当時、佐保から春日地方一帯の機内のかなり広い範囲を支配する相当な力をもった豪族だったと考えられている

revision.comment: /* 沙本之大闇見戸売 */
revision.text: ==概略== [[記紀]]に登場する。[[開化天皇]]の皇子・[[日子坐王]]の妻、[[狭穂彦王]]、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃「[[狭穂姫]]」の母 彼女の母、[[建国勝戸女]]は、春日の建国勝戸女と呼ばれており、この大闇見戸女が率いる狭穂氏は当時、佐保から春日地方一帯の機内のかなり広い範囲を支配する相当な力をもった豪族だったと考えられている
==概略== [[記紀]]に登場する。[[開化天皇]]の皇子・[[彦坐王]]の妻、[[狭穂彦王]]、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃「[[狭穂姫]]」の母 彼女の母、[[建国勝戸女]]は、春日の建国勝戸女と呼ばれており、この大闇見戸女が率いる狭穂氏は当時、佐保から春日地方一帯の機内のかなり広い範囲を支配する相当な力をもった豪族だったと考えられている
==概略== [[記紀]]に登場する女性。[[開化天皇]]の皇子「[[彦坐王]]」の妃、[[狭穂彦王]]および[[垂仁天皇]]の皇后「[[狭穂姫]]」の母 彼女の母、[[建国勝戸女]]は、春日の建国勝戸女と呼ばれており、この大闇見戸女が率いる狭穂氏は当時、佐保から春日地方一帯の機内のかなり広い範囲を支配する相当な力をもった豪族だったと考えられている

revision.comment: 出典の明記template貼り付け
sty
カテゴリ修正、闇見神社の所在地を現在の地名に訂正。
/* 鎮座地 */
誤字の訂正
出典の追加等
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2013年5月14日 (火) 18:49 (UTC)}} '''沙本之大闇見戸売'''（さほのおおくらみとめ）は、[[記紀]]に登場する女性。[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]（＝[[開化天皇]]皇子）の妃。子に[[狭穂彦王|沙本毘古王]]・[[袁邪本王|袁耶本王]]・室毘古（むろびこ）王・[[狭穂姫|沙本毘売命]]（＝[[垂仁天皇]]皇后）がいる。
母は春日建国勝戸売（かすがのたけくにかつとめ）と呼ばれており、この一族は春日野から佐保一帯の[[大和盆地]]北部と深い関係をもつ豪族だったと考えられている。 ==鎮座地== * 闇見神社 [[福井県]][[三方上中郡]][[若狭町]]成願寺字手洗水12-7
== 参考文献 == * 塚口義信『邪馬台国と初期ヤマト政権の謎を探る』[[原書房]]、2016年。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さほのおおくらみとめ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性]] [[Category:日本の神 (人物神 記紀)]]

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and {{ill|Saho no Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫}} (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Women before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese deities (personages in Kojiki and Nihon Shoki)]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]]
[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]]

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王|12=draft}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古|12=draft}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野|12=draft}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地|12=draft}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町|12=draft}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2013年5月14日 (火) 18:49 (UTC)}} '''沙本之大闇見戸売'''（さほのおおくらみとめ）は、[[記紀]]に登場する女性。[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]（＝[[開化天皇]]皇子）の妃。子に[[狭穂彦王|沙本毘古王]]・[[袁邪本王|袁耶本王]]・室毘古（むろびこ）王・[[狭穂姫|沙本毘売命]]（＝[[垂仁天皇]]皇后）がいる。
母は春日建国勝戸売（かすがのたけくにかつとめ）と呼ばれており、この一族は春日野から佐保一帯の[[大和盆地]]北部と深い関係をもつ豪族だったと考えられている。 ==鎮座地== * 闇見神社 [[福井県]][[三方上中郡]][[若狭町]]成願寺字手洗水12-7
== 参考文献 == * 塚口義信『邪馬台国と初期ヤマト政権の謎を探る』[[原書房]]、2016年。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さほのおおくらみとめ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性]] [[Category:日本の神 (人物神 記紀)]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ozaho no Ōkimi|lt=Ozaho no Ōkimi|lt=Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onyaho no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga|qq=|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513714}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Basin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奈良盆地|1=Nara Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942269}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa, Fukui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|children={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13360104]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ozaho no Ōkimi|lt=Ozaho no Ōkimi|lt=Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onyaho no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513714}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Basin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奈良盆地|1=Nara Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942269}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamimi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa, Fukui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513714}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Basin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奈良盆地|1=Nara Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942269}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamimi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa, Fukui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|children={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13360104]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ozaho no Ōkimi|lt=Ozaho no Ōkimi|lt=Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onyaho no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513714}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Basin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奈良盆地|1=Nara Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942269}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamimi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa, Fukui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|version=87539439|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]]

Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513714}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Basin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奈良盆地|1=Nara Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942269}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamimi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa, Fukui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:沙本之大闇見戸売]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|spouse={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}|children={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754|1=Saho-hime}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514|1=Ozaho no Ōkimi}}}} {{Citation needed|date=May 14, 2013}} '''Saho no Ōkurami Tome''' (沙本之大闇見戸売) is a female figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} (Prince Hikoimasu, son of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}). She bore the following children: {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ozaho no Ōkimi|lt=Ozaho no Ōkimi|lt=Onyaho no Mikoto|ja|袁邪本王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onyaho no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[Saho-hime]] (later Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}).
Her mother was known as Kasuga no Takekuni Katsutome (春日建国勝戸売), a name that suggests her lineage was closely tied to the {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga|1=Q11513714|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513714}} and Saho regions, encompassing the northern part of the {{ill|Yamato Basin|ja|大和盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Basin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奈良盆地|1=Nara Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942269}}. This indicates she likely belonged to a powerful local clan in that area.
== Shrine Location ==
* {{ill|Yamimi Shrine|ja|闇見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamimi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|ja|福井県三方上中郡若狭町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa, Fukui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (12-7, Tearuimizu, Jōganji, Wakasa, Fukui Prefecture).
== References == * Tsukaguchi, Yoshinobu. *Exploring the Mysteries of Yamatai and Early Yamato Authority*. Harashobo, 2016.
